questionHtml:
Torre Guaceto is a marine protected area which covers over 22 km2 of the Adriatic Sea in south-eastern Italy. The diagram shows a food chain within this area.

The data refers to the numbers of these three species inside the marine protected area, where fishing is limited, and outside the marine protected area, where fishing is allowed.

[Source: data provided by The Science of Marine Reserves Project (PISCO), based on Guidetti 2006 Ecological Applications]

Compare and contrast the community structure within and outside the marine protected area.
markschemeHtml:
compare:

a. all three species present in both
OR
richness is the same ✔

b. sea urchin numbers highest in both
OR
sea bream density less than sea urchin density in both ✔ Allow converse answer

c. all species numbers are affected by fishing ✔

contrast:

d. sea urchin population is greater outside the marine protected area ✔

e. reduction in sea bream/sea grass percent cover outside the marine protected area ✔

f. less species evenness outside the marine protected area ✔

meta.title: Compare and contrast the community structure within and outside the marine protected area.
breadcrumbLabels: Option C: Ecology and conservation (Core topics)
